vitals.date_specific: 2026: Start Saturday May 23 at 18:00; finish deadline Wednesday May 27 at 22:00 (100-hour clock)
vitals.field_size: Sold out for 2026 (exact rider count not publicly disclosed by the organizer)
vitals.start_format: Wave start, Utrecht, Saturday 18:00-18:35 (per official 2026 start list)
vitals.feed_zones: Self-supported; commercial services only (no organizer-staffed feed zones on the free-routed sections)
vitals.cutoff_time: 100 hours: start Saturday 18:00, finish deadline Wednesday 22:00

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 63. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 60.

course_description.character: Utrecht Ultra is a self-supported, self-navigated road ultra: a mass start from Utrecht at 18:00 on a Saturday, a 100-hour clock, and a route that is only partly fixed. The organizer publishes five fixed parcours — the Utrecht start/finish loop plus three checkpoint parcours in Belgium (an Oudenaarde-area section in the Flemish Ardennes, a section toward Dinant, and a section between La Roche-en-Ardenne and Houffalize) — that together make up roughly 60% of the advertised ~1,000km/6,000m route. Riders plan and ride their own connecting roads between those fixed sections, so no two finishers cover an identical course; the ~1,000km figure is an advertised total, not a fixed measured distance. The surface is almost entirely paved (organizer estimate ~98%). There are no staffed feed zones outside the fixed parcours — riders rely on commercial services (shops, hotels, bakeries) for the free-routed stretches.
course_description.signature_challenge: Self-navigating the roughly 40% of the route that isn't fixed — plotting your own roads between the Oudenaarde, Dinant, and La Roche-Houffalize checkpoint parcours inside a hard 100-hour clock, with no organizer support once you're off the published sections.
suffering_zones: The Oudenaarde-area parcours — Flemish Ardennes terrain, the same rolling hill country the cobbled Classics use, ridden as a self-timed checkpoint section rather than a marked race stage
The La Roche-en-Ardenne to Houffalize parcours — deep into the Belgian Ardennes, among the hillier of the three checkpoint sections
The final push back into Utrecht against the closing 100-hour clock, after several hundred kilometers of self-navigated connecting roads with no organizer support

logistics.lodging_strategy: Self-supported; use commercial services only (hotels, shops) along both the fixed parcours and the free-routed sections.

history.origin_story: Founded in 2023 by Ruben Plat as a shorter, more "achievable" entry into ultra-distance road cycling — roughly 1,000km rather than the 1,900km+ full loop of Race Around the Netherlands. The organizer has since added a longer variant, Utrecht Ultra XL (scheduled Aug 1-7, 2026), alongside the original event.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Distinct, Real Ultra — Not a Gran Fondo, and Not Race Around the Netherlands
biased_opinion.summary: Utrecht Ultra is an organized self-supported road ultra founded in 2023 by Ruben Plat, with its own website, published start list, and a Follow My Challenge tracker used as the event's official timing/results supplier. It is not a duplicate or alias of Race Around the Netherlands (RATN): different organizer, different start city, different dates (Utrecht Ultra runs May 23-27; RATN's 2026 edition ran May 2-10), a much shorter advertised distance (~1,000km vs. RATN's ~1,950km full loop), and a shorter cutoff (100 hours vs. RATN's 202 hours). It is not a gran fondo in the conventional sense: there's a wave start and a hard 100-hour clock, and the tracker publishes finish order and times, but there's no age-category classification or prize structure, and roughly 40% of the route is left to each rider to plan and navigate themselves rather than following a marked, marshalled course.
strengths: A distinct, actively organized event with its own infrastructure — official site, published 2026 start list, and a tracker publishing finish order/times — confirmed to have run May 23-27, 2026
Differentiated from Race Around the Netherlands on organizer, distance, dates, and cutoff — this is not a duplicate profile of that event
Registration for 2026 sold out, though the organizer doesn't publish an exact field-size cap so it's unclear how large that field actually is
The organizer has added a longer Utrecht Ultra XL variant (Aug 1-7, 2026) alongside the original event since its 2023 launch
weaknesses: Not a scored gran fondo — the tracker shows finish order and time, but there's no age-category classification or prize structure, and since roughly 40% of the route is self-planned, individual finishers' routes and total distances aren't identical
The advertised ~1,000km/6,000m figures are round, promotional numbers, not a single fixed measured course — actual distance ridden varies by each rider's routing choices between checkpoints
Self-supported logistics (no staffed feed zones on the free-routed sections) mean riders depend on ordinary commercial services for food and shelter across two countries
Thin track record — only its fourth edition in 2026, with coverage limited to cycling-specific outlets (WielerFlits) and the organizer's own tracker rather than broader independent media
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate, distinct self-supported road ultra for riders who want a shorter multi-day challenge through the Low Countries and Belgian Ardennes — but it belongs in the ultra-distance/bikepacking-race conversation, not alongside conventional scored gran fondos.

final_verdict.one_liner: Self-supported 1,000km road ultra linking Utrecht to fixed Flemish Ardennes and Belgian Ardennes checkpoint parcours within a 100-hour clock.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Consider it if you want a shorter, more achievable entry into self-supported ultra-distance road racing and don't need a ranked competitive field or full course support. Skip it if you're looking for a scored gran fondo experience — this is closer to a bikepacking race with a mass start.
final_verdict.alternatives: Race Around the Netherlands (same country, same self-supported ultra format, roughly double the distance and cutoff, entirely fixed-loop rather than partly free-routed) for riders who want the longer, harder version. Utrecht Ultra XL (same organizer, Aug 1-7, 2026) for a longer variant of this exact concept.

catalog_flags.status_note: fondo_rating.discipline is 'gran_fondo' but this is actually a self-supported, self-navigated ultra-distance road race (~1,000km/6,000m, 100-hour clock, ~40% free-routed by each rider) — closer to a bikepacking-style ultra than a conventional supported gran fondo. Doesn't cleanly fit dimensions like organization, field_depth, or prestige, which assume a fixed, marshalled course. Identity-checked and confirmed NOT the same event as Race Around the Netherlands (different organizer, dates, distance, and cutoff) — see eligibility.notes.

eligibility.notes: IDENTITY CHECK (2026-07-24): Utrecht Ultra is a real, distinct event founded 2023 by Ruben Plat (per WielerFlits), with its own official site, 2026 start list, and a Follow My Challenge tracker used as its official timing/results supplier. Confirmed NOT a duplicate of or alias for Race Around the Netherlands — different organizer (Utrecht Ultra/Ruben Plat vs. Adventure Bike Racing/Michael Wacker), different start city (Utrecht vs. Amerongen), different 2026 dates (May 23-27 vs. May 2-10), different advertised distance (~1,000km/6,000m vs. RATN's ~1,950km/7,000m full loop), and different cutoff (100 hours vs. 202 hours). Both events are tracked separately by third-party ultra-cycling calendars (e.g. Apidura's 2026 events list). 2026 edition confirmed to have run (sold out registration, live tracker results). Purged youtube_data entirely — all 5 prior video entries were about unrelated events (a generic GCN ultra-training tutorial, the Tuscany Trail bikepacking event in Italy, a Dutch criterium race, a Scotland-based ultra documentary, and the separate Flatlands 300 Dutch gravel ultra) and none were about Utrecht Ultra specifically.
